name: Jackie McLean
description: American jazz saxophonist, composer, bandleader, and educator (1931–2006)
abstract: John Lenwood McLean (May 17, 1931 – March 31, 2006) was an American jazz alto saxophonist, composer, bandleader, and educator. He is one of the few musicians to be elected to the DownBeat Hall of Fame in the year of their death.

interviews.title: Jackie McLean — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

concerts.label: Kool Jazz Festival: The Blakey Legacy
JVC Jazz Festival: To Dizzy With Love
Carnegie Hall Jazz Band: The Legacy of Miles
Carnegie Hall Salutes the Jazz Masters: Verve Records at 50

quotes.text: No, because they were both peers. In fact I understand that Bud got a lot of stuff from Elmo. Elmo was the first to play a lot of things that Bud gets credit for. And they used to play classical together at Carnegie Hall, recitals and stuff like that. So they were very close as young guys together.
quotes.caption: Recalling Bud Powell and Elmo Hope playing classical at Carnegie Hall
quotes.doc_title: Jackie McLean — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bobby Hutcherson
about_quotes.evidence: Jackie says, 'I got two weeks at the Club Coronet in Brooklyn... Let’s go into the Club Coronet and play two weeks.' ... So Jackie calls Alfred Lyons. Alfred Lyons comes down. Alfred says, 'Jackie, we have to record this.'
about_quotes.doc_title: Bobby Hutcherson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Mcbee
about_quotes.evidence: Number one my second group — my first group that I performed with in New York City was with Jackie McLean. He trained Bobby Hutcherson, Charles Tolliver, and let me see, and myself.

about_quotes.speaker: Sam Jones
about_quotes.evidence: The last trip I made over there with Art Farmer and Jackie McLean and the three of us, you know, Cedar, and Billy Higgins. That was the last tour

about_quotes.speaker: Tolliver
about_quotes.evidence: my debut recording with him... I was sent to Jackie McLean... he says, 'Okay I’m putting you on my next record date.'

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: I started to work with Jackie and we went to Pittsburgh, did some gigs... I played with Jackie for a while
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Tyner McCoy
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee mentions Jackie McLean only in the context of the album 'It's About Time' and a previous mention by 'Aisha'. The interviewer provides details about McLean's history with Dizzy Gillespie, but the interviewee does not describe a personal or professional relationship with McLean.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tyner McCoy — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
